UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores non-authentication related user data and preferences, linked to an external authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

externalAuthId.description: Reference to the unique identifier from the external authentication system.

username.description: Display name of the user within the application.

email.description: User's contact email address, primarily for communication (not authentication).

role.description: The role of the user, which determines their permissions.

preferences.description: JSON string storing user-specific application settings or preferences.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Ebook.title: Ebook
Ebook.description: Represents a single ebook within the application, including its metadata and content structure.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ebook entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or created this ebook. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Ebook)

authorName.description: The name of the author of the ebook.

description.description: A brief synopsis or description of the ebook content.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the cover image of the ebook.

publicationDate.description: The date when the ebook was published or last updated.

genre.description: The primary genre or category of the ebook.

status.description: The current publication status of the ebook (e.g., 'draft', 'published', 'private').

language.description: The language in which the ebook is written (e.g., 'en', 'id').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ebook record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ebook record was last updated.

Chapter.title: Chapter
Chapter.description: Represents an individual chapter or section within an ebook.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Chapter entity.

ebookId.description: Reference to the Ebook this chapter belongs to. (Relationship: Ebook 1:N Chapter)

content.description: The rich text content of the chapter, potentially in a format like HTML or Markdown.

order.description: The sequential order of the chapter within its ebook.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the chapter was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the chapter was last updated.

Collection.title: Collection
Collection.description: Represents a user-defined collection or grouping of ebooks.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Collection entity.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this collection. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Collection)

name.description: The name of the collection (e.g., 'Favorites', 'Sci-Fi Reads').

description.description: A brief description of the collection's purpose or content.

ebookIds.description: An array of IDs referencing the Ebooks included in this collection. (Relationship: Collection N:N Ebook)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the collection was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the collection was last updated.

ReadingProgress.title: ReadingProgress
ReadingProgress.description: Tracks a user's reading progress for a specific ebook.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReadingProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ReadingProgress)

ebookId.description: Reference to the Ebook for which the progress is being tracked. (Relationship: Ebook 1:N ReadingProgress)

currentChapterId.description: Reference to the Chapter the user is currently reading. (Relationship: Chapter 1:N ReadingProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The reading progress as a percentage (0.0 to 1.0) within the current chapter or entire book.

lastReadAt.description: Timestamp of the last time the user engaged with this ebook.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the reading progress record was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the reading progress record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores non-authentication related user data and preferences. Documents are named by their `userId` (matching `request.auth.uid`) and are private to the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual ebook metadata and details owned by a specific user. The `ownerId` field in the document must match the path's `userId` and `request.auth.uid` for writes. For reads, owners can access all statuses; other users can read only if the `status` field is 'published', supporting QAPs via collection group queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the ebook.

params.name: ebookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the ebook.

definition.description: Stores individual chapters for a specific ebook. Each chapter document explicitly includes denormalized `ebookOwnerId` (matching the parent ebook's owner) and `ebookStatus` (matching the parent ebook's publication status) to ensure Authorization Independence. This allows security rules to evaluate chapter access without fetching the parent ebook, supporting QAPs for published chapters.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent ebook.

params.name: ebookId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent ebook.

definition.description: Stores user-defined collections of ebooks. Each collection is private to the owning user; the `ownerId` field in the document must match the path's `userId` and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the collection.

params.name: collectionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the collection.

definition.description: Tracks a user's reading progress for specific ebooks. Each progress record is private to the user; the `userId` field in the document must match the path's `userId` and `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user whose reading progress is being tracked.

params.name: readingProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the reading progress record (e.g., a combination of user and ebook ID, or a generated ID).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This is achieved primarily through explicit denormalization. For example, `Chapter` documents, which reside as a subcollection of `Ebook`s, will explicitly store `ebookOwnerId` (the ID of the Ebook's owner) and `ebookStatus` (the Ebook's publication status). This means that security rules for `Chapter` documents can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid` against `chapter.ebookOwnerId` and check `chapter.ebookStatus` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `Ebook` document. This eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies, enabling atomic operations (like creating an Ebook and its first Chapter in a single batch) and simplifying rule debugging.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
All user-owned and private data (Ebooks, Chapters, Collections, ReadingProgress) are segregated under `/users/{userId}/` paths. This ensures that documents within these collections share a common security posture: they are primarily accessible by the `userId` specified in the path, which must match `request.auth.uid`. 

*   **Private Data:** `UserProfile` data is stored in a top-level `user_profiles` collection, accessible only by the owning user (matching `request.auth.uid` to the document ID). User-specific entities like `Collections` and `ReadingProgress` are stored directly under `/users/{userId}/` paths, ensuring `request.auth.uid` matches the path's `userId` and the document's `ownerId` or `userId` field.

*   **Public/Published Content (QAPs):** For `Ebook`s and `Chapter`s, which can have a 'published' status, read access is extended to other users. The `/users/{userId}/ebooks/{ebookId}` structure allows the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`) to access all their ebooks (drafts, private, published). For global discoverability and listing of *published* ebooks by *any* user, security rules will permit `list` operations via collection group queries on `ebooks` subcollections where `resource.data.status == 'published'`. Similarly, `Chapter` documents, due to denormalized `ebookStatus`, can be read by any user if `chapter.ebookStatus == 'published'`. This approach fully supports QAPs, allowing efficient and secure `list` operations without relying on slow or insecure filters.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** The primary model for user-specific data, such as `/users/{userId}/ebooks/{ebookId}` and `/users/{userId}/collections/{collectionId}`, makes ownership-based security rules extremely simple (`request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **Hierarchical Paths:** Nested data like `Chapter`s follow the hierarchy `/users/{userId}/ebooks/{ebookId}/chapters/{chapterId}`, maintaining context and simplifying related data access.
*   **DBAC:** All authorization is based on `request.auth.uid` matching data in the database (e.g., `ownerId`, `userId` fields, or path wildcards).

This design prioritizes a flat rule structure, robust authorization independence via denormalization where necessary, and clear access patterns for all data types.
